content: The intrinsic reward signal generated by an Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) can be reframed as a dynamic 'memory pruning trigger' for active learning agents: whenever the ICM detects a large prediction error between an agent’s expected and actual state transitions, it flags that unexpected experience as high-value for long-term retention while filtering out predictable, redundant data that does not improve the agent’s world model. This creates a self-organizing memory cycle where curiosity not only drives exploration but also keeps the agent’s working and long-term memory efficient without external reward supervision.
